tools.description: Control mouse actions with schema-guided coordinate targeting.

button.description: Mouse button for click, double_click, and drag actions

drag_to_x.description: Destination X coordinate for drag actions

drag_to_y.description: Destination Y coordinate for drag actions

duration.description: Duration in seconds for drag operations

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

wait.description: Delay in seconds before taking a screenshot after tool execution.

tools.name: mouse_control

button.description: Mouse button for click, double_click, and drag actions

candidate_id.description: Stable OCR candidate id from an earlier ambiguity response.

destination_description.description: Detailed visual description of the drag destination for prediction targeting.

drag_to_candidate_id.description: Stable OCR candidate id for drag destination targeting.

drag_to_model_name.description: Optional specific vision model for drag destination prediction.

drag_to_ocr_text.description: Exact visible on-screen text for drag destination OCR targeting.

drag_to_x.description: Destination X coordinate in captured-image pixels for drag actions. Required when drag_to_find_coordinates_by='manual'.

drag_to_y.description: Destination Y coordinate in captured-image pixels for drag actions. Required when drag_to_find_coordinates_by='manual'.

duration.description: Duration in seconds for drag operations

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

model_name.description: Optional specific vision model for prediction targeting.

ocr_text.description: Exact visible on-screen text for OCR targeting.

source_description.description: Detailed visual description of the source target for prediction targeting.

wait.description: Delay in seconds before taking a screenshot after tool execution.

x.description: X coordinate in captured-image pixels. Required when find_coordinates_by='manual'. Beware of the mouse position on the image when determining manual coordinates.

y.description: Y coordinate in captured-image pixels. Required when find_coordinates_by='manual'. Beware of the mouse position on the image when determining manual coordinates.

tools.description: Control keyboard input including typing text, clipboard paste, pressing keys, and shortcuts. After input, verify text appears in the latest captured screen image; do not assume tool success means input landed. If text is missing, refocus the field and retry. Use deterministic action sequences for predictable flows (for example, input text then press Enter only when submit is intended). Prefer this tool over mouse clicks when a shortcut or key-driven path exists.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

key.description: Single key to press (required for 'press' action)

keys.description: List of keys for hotkey (required for 'hotkey' action)

repeat.description: Repeat count for press or hotkey actions.

text.description: Text to input (required for 'type' and 'paste' actions)

wait.description: Delay in seconds before taking a screenshot after tool execution.

tools.name: keyboard_control

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

keys.description: List of keys for hotkey (required for 'hotkey' action)

repeat.description: Repeat count for press or hotkey actions.

text.description: Text to input (required for 'type' and 'paste' actions)

wait.description: Delay in seconds before taking a screenshot after tool execution.

tools.description: Capture a screenshot of the current computer screen.

display_bounds.description: (OPTIONAL) Display bounds to capture instead of the full desktop.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

wait.description: (OPTIONAL) Delay in seconds before capturing a screenshot. If provided, waits this duration before capture.

tools.name: screenshot

display_bounds.description: (OPTIONAL) Display bounds to capture instead of the full desktop.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

wait.description: (OPTIONAL) Delay in seconds before capturing a screenshot. If provided, waits this duration before capture.

tools.description: Control desktop scrolling actions. Target the scroll region using the currently enabled grounding fields exposed by this schema. Prefer 'manual' as it's less compute-heavy. Omit `clicks` on the first vertical scroll attempt so the executor uses its default click amount; use `clicks` only for follow-up fine tuning.

executable_schema.description:
Arguments for scroll control tool. Vertical: up/down (vscroll). Horizontal: left/right (hscroll).

Vertical scroll defaults are executor-owned OS-default literal click counts.
Optional `clicks` remains available as a literal override.

clicks.description: Optional explicit literal OS wheel click override. Fallback-only for follow-up fine tuning. Omit it on the first vertical scroll attempt so the executor chooses the default click amount (8 on macOS, 5 on Windows/Linux). Provide it only when a smaller or larger manual adjustment is needed.

direction.description: Direction for scroll action: vertical 'up'|'down', or horizontal 'left'|'right'. Required when action is 'scroll'.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

wait.description: Delay in seconds before taking a screenshot after tool execution.

x.description: X coordinate to move to before scrolling (manual coordinates only)

y.description: Y coordinate to move to before scrolling (manual coordinates only)

tools.name: scroll_control

tools.description: Wait for a specified number of seconds, then capture a fresh image of the current screen state. Useful for waiting for UI changes, animations, page loads, or async operations to complete. After execution, returns a status message and the captured image.

seconds.description: Number of seconds to wait before capturing a screenshot.

tools.description:
Launch a GUI app as a detached desktop process so it remains running after the current agent turn ends.

Verification modes:
- window (default): polls open windows for expected title.
- screenshot: captures visual proof after launch and returns capture artifact fields.
- none: fastest acknowledgment without verification.

Use this for open-and-leave-running app workflows.

executable_schema.description: Arguments for detached app launch tool.

args.description: (OPTIONAL) Positional arguments for the app launch command.

command.description: Executable or app command to launch

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

verify.description: (OPTIONAL) Post-launch verification mode: none (no verification), window (poll open windows), screenshot (capture screenshot evidence).

verify_timeout_seconds.description: (OPTIONAL) Max seconds to wait for verification.

verify_window_title.description: (OPTIONAL) Window title substring to verify after launch.

tools.name: open_app

schema.description: Arguments for detached app launch tool.

args.description: (OPTIONAL) Positional arguments for the app launch command.

command.description: Executable or app command to launch

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

verify.description: (OPTIONAL) Post-launch verification mode: none (no verification), window (poll open windows), screenshot (capture screenshot evidence).

verify_timeout_seconds.description: (OPTIONAL) Max seconds to wait for verification.

verify_window_title.description: (OPTIONAL) Window title substring to verify after launch.

tools.description:
This tool executes shell commands on the local system. Commands are executed in the specified directory (or the selected workspace folder when configured, otherwise the OS user home directory if not specified).

Execution Modes:
- Foreground (run_in_background=False): Blocks until command completion and returns output. Use terminate_after_seconds to set a timeout (default 120 seconds). If timeout is reached, the command is terminated and current output is returned. Backend-owned history projection handles any model-facing truncation.
- Background (run_in_background=True): Starts the command and returns immediately with execution confirmation. Does not block for output or completion.
- Yield (yield_after_seconds): Returns early if the command runs longer than the yield time; the command continues in the background and can be managed through the returned session.

Operational Guidance:
- Prefer short commands focused on one step per tool call.
- Do not embed large inline file content (HTML/JSON/source blobs) directly in shell command arguments.
- For creating or updating file contents, prefer dedicated file-edit capabilities before running shell commands.
- Split large workflows into multiple tool calls rather than one giant command payload.
- For detached GUI app launches, prefer the dedicated app-launch capability when appropriate.
- For shell jobs you need to poll or terminate, keep run_in_background=True and manage through the returned session.
- After launch, capture a delayed screen image when visual verification matters.
- Use exact known window titles and deterministic focus checks when window targeting matters.

Optional post-execution delay: when provided, the tool pauses for that many seconds and captures a screen image after execution. This is useful when the command opens a GUI application or makes visual changes that need to be captured.

Returns: Command output, exit code, execution time, and any errors.

command.description: Command to execute. For repository or log search, prefer fast targeted commands such as rg instead of broad recursive grep, and exclude generated directories like node_modules, frontend/release, frontend/python-runtime, and .git unless the user explicitly needs them.

directory.description: Working directory. Absolute paths are allowed, and relative paths resolve from the selected workspace folder when configured, otherwise from the OS user home directory. If omitted, the tool uses that default base directory directly.

env.description: (OPTIONAL) Environment variable overrides for the command.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

wait.description: (OPTIONAL) Delay in seconds before taking a screenshot after tool execution. If provided, the tool will wait and capture a screenshot like computer-use tools.

yield_after_seconds.description: (OPTIONAL) Return early if command runs longer than this (seconds). The command continues in the background.

action.description: Action to perform: list, poll, log, write, send-keys, submit, paste, kill, clear, remove.

data.description: Data to write for write action

session_id.description: Session id for actions other than list/clear

tools.description: Read file contents. Use this tool to examine existing files.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

file_path.description: Path to the file to read. Absolute paths are allowed, and relative paths resolve from the selected workspace folder when available; otherwise they resolve from the OS user home directory.

limit.description: Maximum amount to read (defaults to 2000 when omitted). For text files this is max lines; for PDF files this is max pages considered before size-aware selection.

offset.description: 0-based offset to start reading from (defaults to 0). For text files this is a line offset; for PDF files this is a page offset.

tools.name: read_file

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

limit.description: Maximum amount to read (defaults to 2000 when omitted). For text files this is max lines; for PDF files this is max pages considered before size-aware selection.

offset.description: 0-based offset to start reading from (defaults to 0). For text files this is a line offset; for PDF files this is a page offset.

tools.description:
Replace text in a file using exact or context-anchored matching. Supports one or more edits through replacements[].

Operational Guidance:
- Prefer focused edits per tool call.
- Do not send giant new_string payloads in one call.
- For large changes, split them into multiple focused edit calls section-by-section.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

file_path.description: Path to the file to modify. Absolute paths are allowed, and relative paths resolve from the selected workspace folder when available; otherwise they resolve from the OS user home directory.

patch_chunks.description: Optional apply_patch-style ordered update chunks. Cannot be combined with replacements. Prefer multiple focused chunks/calls over one oversized payload.

replacements.description: Optional replacement operations applied atomically. Use a one-item list for a single edit.

tools.name: replace

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

patch_chunks.description: Optional apply_patch-style ordered update chunks. Cannot be combined with replacements. Prefer multiple focused chunks/calls over one oversized payload.

replacements.description: Optional replacement operations applied atomically. Use a one-item list for a single edit.

tools.description: Control the dedicated browser instance for navigation, extraction, page interaction, tab management, and screenshots.

executable_schema.description: Canonical grouped browser action payload. Provide a top-level explanation and the fields for the selected action only. Action-specific field requirements are enforced by runtime validation.

activate.description: Bring the selected browser tab to the foreground. Set to false to switch the agent's internal control target without changing the user-visible active tab.

attributes.description: Attributes to include in the results.

context_chars.description: Context characters to include around each match.

css_scope.description: Optional CSS scope for the search.

description.description: Optional note about the navigation step.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

extract_links.description: Include page links in extracted source text before filtering.

anyOf.description: Optional output filename for the screenshot. / Optional output PDF file name under the browser file store.

file_name.description: Optional output filename for the screenshot. / Optional output PDF file name under the browser file store. / Browser-side file name. / Browser-side file name. / Browser-side file name.

files_to_display.description: Optional attachment paths to surface with the completion result.

include_screenshot.description: Include a screenshot with the snapshot result when available.

include_text.description: Include element text in the results.

anyOf.description: Actionable Browser Use element index from the latest snapshot output.
Actionable Browser Use element index from the latest snapshot output.

index.description: Actionable Browser Use element index from the latest snapshot output.

leading_newline.description: Insert a leading newline before the written content.

limit.description: Maximum number of snapshot characters to return.

max_results.description: Maximum number of matches to return. / Maximum number of results to return. / Maximum number of matches to return.

new_tab.description: Open the URL in a new tab.

offset.description: Character offset into the snapshot text for paginated reads.

output_schema.description: Optional JSON schema hint for structured extraction output.

pages.description: Optional page-count override for Browser Use scrolling.

path.description: Path to the file to upload.

pattern.description: Pattern to find on the page.

query.description: Extraction goal or question. / Search query.

regex.description: Interpret the pattern as a regex.

seconds.description: Seconds to wait. Omit to use the runtime default wait.

start_from_char.description: Character offset into extracted page content for long pages.

text.description: Text to type. / Completion text to return. / Text to find. / Visible option text to select.

trailing_newline.description: Append a trailing newline after the written content.
